markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Represent knowledge graph writes from agents as proposals that can be reviewed, merged, rejected, or promoted. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: GraphMutationProposalModel leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: GraphMutationProposalModel is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 c418249f; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: Proposals are correctly DRAFT (authority_class='support', NEVER authority; 0152:90). decide_graph_proposal is an atomic conditional UPDATE 'WHERE review_state=''proposed''' (knowledge_crdt.rs:709-739) so concurrent double-decisions cannot both win -- the second matches 0 rows -> None. Reviewer regex CHECK forbids model self-approval (0152:73-77 '^(operator|validator):'). Decision fields travel together (chk ..._decision, 0152:63-71). State machine is sound.

SOFT-REF ORIGIN (medium, root cause of the MT-069 authority hole): source_span_refs accepts ARBITRARY non-empty strings. insert_graph_proposal (knowledge_crdt.rs:637-646) only rejects empty/blank entries; the migration CHECK only requires a non-empty JSON array (0152:57-61). The 0152 header explicitly says 'Soft refs by design ... the promotion gate (MT-069) re-validates' -- but that re-validation does NOT exist in MT-069. So this MT is the entry point of the dangling-evidence authority hole: span strings inserted here are never validated against knowledge_spans anywhere in the pipeline.

Lease gating is PRESENCE-ONLY, not validity. validate_graph_proposal_request DOES reject a model actor with lease_id=None (graph_proposal.rs:180-184 ModelActorWithoutLease), and the FK (0152:40) ensures the lease row exists. But neither the validator nor the insert calls guard_lease_for_write, so an EXPIRED, RELEASED, FOREIGN-actor, or wrong-SCOPE lease id satisfies the rule as long as the row exists. Same existence-only weakness as MT-074/MT-079.

actor_id/actor_kind/session_id/correlation_id are all NOT NULL with regex/non-empty CHECKs (0152:33-38) -- the spec actor/state-vector/span identification MUSTs are structurally enforced for the proposal record.
evasion_probes: Inserted a proposal citing source_span_refs=['pending:ghost:0-1']: accepted (non-empty string passes). No knowledge_spans existence check. This is the seam that becomes authority at MT-069.
Tried double-decide a proposal: second decide_graph_proposal returns None (conditional UPDATE) -> no lost-update double decision.
Tried model self-approval via decided_by='local_model:x': blocked by reviewer regex CHECK at the DB layer.
Tried promoting a 'proposed' (un-approved) proposal directly: MT-069 denies NotApproved and mark_graph_proposal_promoted also conditions on review_state='approved' (knowledge_crdt.rs:742-758).
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0152_knowledge_crdt_graph_proposals.sql:18-91 (schema, CHECKs, soft-ref-by-design comment, registry support class)

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG negative test green (33 CRDT tests pass under runtime-full,test-utils).

evidence: Hardened d6adf2dd: Graph-proposal lease chokepoint + span-string validation feed into the promotion guard.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
